subheadline: Industry leaders choose to Build with Devin
value_proposition: An autonomous AI software engineer that handles complex, repetitive, and large-scale engineering tasks—from code migrations to bug triage—at a fraction of the cost and time of human engineers.
target_audience: Enterprise engineering teams and technical leaders at large organizations dealing with legacy codebases, large-scale migrations, or high volumes of repetitive engineering work.

testimonials: Devin provided an easy way to reduce the number of engineering hours for the migration, in a way that was more stable and less prone to human error. Rather than engineers having to work across several files and complete an entire migration task 100%, they could just review Devin's changes, make minor adjustments, then merge their PR — Jose Carlos Castro, Senior Product Manager, Nubank
metrics: 40 minutes per sub-task dropped to 10 after fine-tuning
2x improvement in task completion scores after fine-tuning

pricing.price_anchoring: Frames cost savings as 20x cheaper than human engineer hourly rates; additional value emphasized as completing projects months ahead of schedule

features: PR review and visual QA with automatic bug identification and intelligent diff organization
Auto-generated documentation and system diagrams for legacy codebases
Parallel fleet of agents for large-scale code migrations and refactors (COBOL, .NET, Talend, legacy ETL)
Issue triage and bug fixing via Datadog, Slack, and Linear integrations
Fine-tuning on organization-specific examples to improve task completion speed and accuracy over time
differentiators: Fine-tuning capability on customer-specific codebases and task examples
Fleet-of-agents model for parallelizing large migrations across thousands of sub-tasks
Learns tribal knowledge and gets faster/more reliable with each task (compounding improvement)
Deep integration with the tools engineering teams already use (GitHub, Linear, Slack, Datadog)
Human stays in the loop only for review and approval, not execution
trust_signals: Detailed Nubank enterprise case study with verified quantitative outcomes
Named enterprise tier implying security/compliance controls
Quote attributed to named executive (Jose Carlos Castro, Senior PM at Nubank)
Benchmark evaluation set methodology described for fine-tuning validation

design_patterns.layout: Top nav with dual CTA, large hero section, featured customer case study with metrics, use-case grid, integration logos marquee, enterprise conversion section at bottom
design_patterns.color_scheme: Dark background with high-contrast white text; accent colors likely blue or electric tones (inferred from AI/tech brand conventions and page structure)
design_patterns.typography: Modern sans-serif; bold display type for metrics and headlines
design_patterns.visual_style: Technical and confident; enterprise-grade with clean minimal structure
notable_patterns: Metric callout cards (8x, 20x) as anchor elements
Long-form case study embedded in hero to establish credibility before feature listing
Side-by-side knowledge/workflow UI mockup for 'learns your codebase' section

emotional_tone: confident/aspirational — positions Devin as the obvious solution to a painful, expensive, universal engineering problem; aspirational framing around freeing engineers from drudgework
key_objections_addressed: Is it actually reliable? (addressed via fine-tuning benchmark scores and case study completion rates)
Will engineers lose control? (addressed by emphasizing human-in-the-loop review and PR approval workflow)
Is it too expensive? (addressed with 20x cost savings vs engineer hourly rates)
Can it handle our complex, unique codebase? (addressed via fine-tuning on org-specific examples and tribal knowledge learning)
Will it slow us down to set up? (addressed by framing setup as 'small fixed cost' with compounding returns)
missing_or_weak: No visible pricing page content or starting price — forces contact for even basic cost discovery
Only one customer case study prominently featured (Nubank); needs more diversity of logos and named testimonials
No free trial or sandbox offer — high commitment ask for a technical buyer who wants to evaluate before purchasing
Security and compliance details (SOC 2, data handling, code confidentiality) not surfaced on the landing page despite enterprise audience
No video demo or interactive product tour — technical buyers typically want to see the product in action before engaging sales
Competitor differentiation is implicit rather than explicit — no direct comparison to GitHub Copilot, Cursor, or other AI coding tools
No FAQ section to address common technical or procurement concerns
standout_elements: Leading with a deeply specific, metrics-rich enterprise case study (Nubank) before any feature list — builds massive credibility immediately
Fine-tuning narrative is a genuine differentiator and is explained clearly with before/after numbers (40min → 10min)
Compounding improvement story ('like a human engineer') creates a compelling long-term value narrative
Fleet-of-agents framing is distinctive and addresses scale in a way competitors don't
Integration ecosystem breadth (18+ named tools) signals enterprise readiness without a dedicated integrations page
'Fewer dreaded migration tasks' as a benefit — speaks directly to engineer sentiment, not just manager ROI
